revision.text:
[[de:Chau (Dekan)]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |TITEL = Chau |NAME = M12-M12-M12 N14-N33:N33:N33:N33:N33-N33:N33:N33:N33:N33-N33:N33:N33:N33:N33-N33:N33:N33-N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Chau
''ẖ3w''
''Thousands'' }} '''Chau''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|qid=Q5248738|lt=Decan|1=Decan}}, which comprised 20 {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qid=Q804527}} (decan stars). {{ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de|Richard-Anthony Parker|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard-Anthony Parker|qid=Q2148992|1=Richard Anthony Parker}} and {{ill|Otto E. Neugebauer|en|Otto E. Neugebauer|qid=Q111996|lt=Otto Neugebauer|1=Otto E. Neugebauer}} identified this {{ill|Star|en|Star|qid=Q523|lt=Star|1=Star}} cluster as a {{ill|Star cluster|en|Star cluster|qid=Q168845|lt=Star cluster|1=Star cluster}}. In the celestial imagery of {{ill|Dendera|en|Dendera|qid=Q29019|lt=Dendera|1=Dendera}}, this decan is depicted as a "kneeling woman," referred to by {{ill|Teucer of Babylon|en|Teucer of Babylon|qid=Q7707029|lt=Teucer of Babylon|1=Teucer of Babylon}} as a "dead woman." In {{ill|The Sacred Book of Hermes to Asclepius|de|Das heilige Buch des Hermes an Askeplios|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Sacred Book of Hermes to Asclepius|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, this decan is associated with a {{ill|Mummy|en|Mummy|qid=Q43616|lt=Mummy|1=Mummy}} in connection to Chau. This decan represents the {{ill|Andromeda Galaxy|de|Andromedanebel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andromeda Galaxy|qid=Q2469|1=Andromeda Galaxy}} (M31), which can be observed with the naked eye on clear nights as a nebula. The Andromeda Galaxy (M31) is located near the star ν {{ill|Andromeda (constellation)|en|Andromeda (constellation)|qid=Q9256|lt=Andromeda (constellation)|1=Andromeda (constellation)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qid=Q2005559|1=Book of Nut}}, Chau represented the 29th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qid=Q165496}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|qid=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising|1=Heliacal rising}} was set for the {{ill|Peret#Dating|lt=16th Peret II|de|16. Peret II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret#Dating|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|qid=Q19248|lt=Senusret III|1=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|qid=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 93. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qid=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62 and 386. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, p. 151. {{translated page|de|Chau (Dekan)|version=157720990|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ja:チャウ]] [[de:Chau (Dekan)]] {{wikidata link|Q1068317}}
